zh-hans.Oxford, Oxfordshire, England: 英国奧可福爾奧可福德

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Wade
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Wade (Old English Wada or, via Norman French, from ancient Germanic Wado, of unclear origin but possibly related to Old English wadan ‘to go’).

English: topographic name from Middle English wade ‘ford’ (Old English (ge)wæd ‘ford, especially a tidal crossing’) for someone who lived by a ford, or a habitational name from a place so called, such as Wade Hall in North Cove (Suffolk).
Irish: shortened and altered from Mac Uaid, see McQuaid .

variants.name: Wadeson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name   Wade  .

variants.name: Wass
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Wace (ancient Germanic Waz(z)o), a pet form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Wad- (compare Wade 1). The name Wace was common among the Normans, alongside the Parisian French variant Gace, and was latinized as Wazo (nominative), Wazonis (genitive).
English (of Norman origin): occasionally, perhaps a variant of Vause with vocalizing of initial V-.
German: nickname from Middle High German was(se) ‘sharp, cutting’.

variants.name: Waide
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Wade  .
German: variant of   Weide  .
Irish: shortened form of Mac Uaid, see McQuaid .

variants.name: Waddy
meanings: English: variant of Wadey, perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Wady, a pet form of Wade , or in northern England alternatively a shortened form of Waldo . The name may also be a variant of Wilday .

variants.name: McQuaid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Colum, Nuala.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaid ‘son of Uad’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Scottish form McQuate .

variants.name: Waithe
meanings: English:
either a topographic name from Old Norse vath ‘ford’, or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Waithe (Lincolnshire) and Wath (North and Yorkshire).
variant of   Wade  .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Henry de la Wade I was born in 1180 as the son of William de la Wade. He had at least 1 son. He died in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Henry de la Wade I est né en 1180, fils de William de la Wade. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé à Oxford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Henry de la Wade I wurde 1180, als Sohn von William de la Wade geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Henry de la Wade I è nato il nel 1180 come figlio di William de la Wade. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel Oxford, Oxfordshire, England.

ja.Oxford, Oxfordshire, England: イギリスオクスフォードシュアイアオクスフォード

ko.lifeSummary: Henry de la Wade I(은)는 1180년 William de la Wade의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국 스퍼드실르 스퍼드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Henry de la Wade I nasceu em 1180 filho de William de la Wade. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Henry de la Wade I родился в 1180 г. Его отца звали William de la Wade. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в местности оксфорд, оксфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Henry de la Wade I nació en 1180 como hijo de William de la Wade. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra.
